format: Mitchell et al. 2019 (Model Cards for Model Reporting)

model_details.name: The Exponential Atlas v6
model_details.type: Coupled dynamical system with Monte Carlo simulation
model_details.description: A computational model of cross-domain technology acceleration that quantifies how 42 technology domains interact and amplify each other's improvement rates.  The model combines historical data fitting (log-linear, piecewise, Wright's Law, logistic) with a coupled dynamical simulation to project technology trajectories from 2026 to 2039 under three scenarios.
model_details.unique_contribution: First open model to quantify recursive cross-domain technology amplification with evidence-based interaction weights, diminishing-returns saturation modeling, and full sensitivity analysis.  Unlike single-domain forecasts, the Atlas captures how breakthroughs in AI accelerate drug discovery which accelerates genomics which feeds back into AI — and quantifies the uncertainty at each step.

primary_uses: Understanding how technology domains amplify each other
Identifying which parameters drive future uncertainty
Educational tool for technology forecasting methodology
out_of_scope: Investment decisions (model has wide uncertainty bands)

data_summary.citation_coverage: 100% of data points have source URLs

critical_parameter.name: AI recursive self-improvement (AI -> AI)
critical_parameter.sensitivity: Changes 2039 AI median by ~3-5x per 0.5 weight change
critical_parameter.justification: Based on NAS (~100x narrow speedup), AlphaChip (10-30% improvement), Copilot (1.3-1.55x productivity). No evidence of general recursive self-improvement.

simulation_parameters.coupling_strength: Adaptive gamma, starts at 0.06, decays based on system state
simulation_parameters.adoption_model: Bass diffusion with domain-specific (p, q) parameters
simulation_parameters.breakthrough_events: Domain-specific probabilities (0.01-0.04), amplified by AI improvement
simulation_parameters.constraints: Physical floor/ceiling, max 100x/year growth, regulatory friction

sensitivity_analysis.note: Sobol analysis requires ~32K simulation evaluations and is not included in quick validation runs.  Run separately via run_sobol_analysis() for full results.
sensitivity_analysis.expected_finding: AI base rate and AI->AI interaction weight are expected to dominate variance in most domains, because AI interacts with nearly every other domain
sensitivity_analysis.structural_vs_data: Base rates (data-driven) are expected to account for ~60% of variance; interaction weights ~30%; structural parameters ~10%

known_limitations.limitation: Several domains have fewer than 6 data points
known_limitations.mitigation: Wider confidence bands, low confidence rating

known_limitations.limitation: Assumes smooth continuous improvement; cannot model step-function breakthroughs
known_limitations.mitigation: Stochastic breakthrough events partially capture this

known_limitations.limitation: Does not model regulatory constraints, geopolitical disruption, resource bottlenecks, social resistance, or black swan events
known_limitations.mitigation: Documented prominently; forward projections labeled as upper bounds

known_limitations.limitation: Cross-domain amplification factors are estimated from limited evidence
known_limitations.mitigation: Every weight has citation + counter-evidence; sensitivity analysis shows impact

known_limitations.limitation: 14-year horizon with compounding uncertainty; 2039 projections have wide bands
known_limitations.mitigation: p10-p90 bands shown; deployed (adoption-adjusted) projections much more conservative

not_modeled: Regulatory constraints and government policy changes
Resource bottlenecks (rare earth minerals, skilled labor, energy)
Black swan events (pandemics, natural disasters, financial crises)
Environmental feedback loops (climate change affecting infrastructure)
ethical_considerations: Projections of rapid technology change may cause anxiety or unrealistic expectations
Model could be misused to justify premature technology investment decisions
Exponential improvement in AI capability raises alignment and safety concerns not captured by cost metrics
Projections of 'free energy' or 'end of work' may obscure distributional justice questions
Technology costs approaching zero does not mean universal access -- deployment barriers are social and political, not just economic
limitations.recommendation: This model is an exploration tool, not an oracle.  Use it to understand possible trajectories and key uncertainties, not to make specific predictions about the future.

criteria.criterion: 8+ data points per domain (5+ for new)
